topic: slope, cold-air drainage, and spring frost risk

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Cold air forms on clear spring nights and moves downslope because it is denser than warm air.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Slopes allow cold air to drain away, whereas valleys and depressions collect it around the vines.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Slope and topography strongly influence frost exposure; well-drained slopes are usually less vulnerable than low-lying sites.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Slope and topography strongly influence frost exposure; well-drained slopes are usually less vulnerable than low-lying sites.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
